ALUNO
BUSINESS ENGLISH — 30 AULAS

Fabiana Michelly Silva

De profissional que entende inglês mas conduz tudo em português — para quem transita em todos os níveis da Bloomberg também em inglês

B2 · Intermediário Superior Supply Chain · Bloomberg 30 aulas · 3x semana 60 min · Online
Progresso Geral0%
Speed:

Welcome, Fabiana!

Before your first lesson, complete the Pre-class activities. Here are five emergency phrases you can use at any moment:

Could you repeat that, please? Poderia repetir, por favor?
I am not sure I understand. Could you explain? Não tenho certeza se entendi. Poderia explicar?
Let me think about that for a moment. Deixa eu pensar nisso por um momento.
I work in the supply chain department at Bloomberg. Trabalho no departamento de supply chain da Bloomberg.
I am responsible for procurement and vendor management. Sou responsável por aquisição e gestão de fornecedores.
Aula 01 -- Pre-class

Diagnostic + Who Is Fabiana? -- Mapping Your English

Supply chain vocabulary audit and diagnostic elicitation. Key words: supply chain, vendor, procurement, stakeholder, compliance, logistics, milestone, forecast, shipment, delivery, leverage, deployment. Structures: present simple, present perfect, past simple.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Supply chain -- the entire system of producing and delivering a product
"We need to optimize our supply chain to reduce costs." (Cadeia de suprimentos)
Vendor -- a company or person that sells goods or services
"Our main vendor is based in Singapore." (Fornecedor)
Procurement -- the process of finding and buying goods for a company
"The procurement team handles all equipment purchases." (Aquisição)
Stakeholder -- a person with an interest or concern in a project
"We briefed all stakeholders before the meeting." (Parte interessada)
Compliance -- following rules, regulations, or standards
"All vendors must meet our compliance requirements." (Conformidade)
Logistics -- the detailed coordination of a complex operation
"The logistics of the rollout were challenging." (Logística)
Milestone -- a significant event or achievement in a project
"Reaching Q3 targets was a major milestone." (Marco)
Forecast -- a prediction of future trends based on data
"The demand forecast looks strong for next quarter." (Previsão)
Shipment -- goods sent from one place to another
"The shipment arrived two days late." (Remessa)
Delivery -- the act of bringing goods to a destination
"We expect delivery by the end of the month." (Entrega)
Leverage -- to use something to maximum advantage
"We can leverage our relationship with the vendor." (Alavancar)
Deployment -- the process of putting resources into use
"The terminal deployment is scheduled for October." (Implantação)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Supply chain
Vendor
Procurement
Stakeholder
Compliance
Logistics
Milestone
Forecast
Shipment
Delivery
Leverage
Deployment

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Fabiana works in the supply chain department at Bloomberg. Every day, she coordinates with vendors and manages procurement for specialized trading equipment. Last quarter, her team handled a critical shipment delay when a logistics partner failed to meet compliance standards. "We have dealt with similar situations before," she explains. "The key is having a solid forecast and keeping all stakeholders informed. I have worked at Bloomberg for several years, and every milestone we reach teaches us something about leveraging our vendor relationships for better delivery outcomes."

1. Which tense does Fabiana use to describe her daily routine?
A Present simple: "works", "coordinates", "manages"
B Present perfect: "has worked", "has coordinated"
C Past simple: "worked", "coordinated"
2. Which tense describes the shipment delay as a completed past event?
A Present perfect: "have handled"
B Past simple: "handled", "failed"
C Present simple: "handles", "fails"
3. Why does Fabiana say "We have dealt with similar situations" using the present perfect?
A Because it happened at a specific time last year.
B Because it is happening right now.
C Because the experience connects past actions to the present moment.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Tense Review (B2 Diagnostic)

GRAMMAR

Revisão dos 3 tempos verbais presentes no texto. / Review of the 3 tenses found in the text.

Tense / TempoUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
Present SimpleRotinas, fatos permanentes / Routines, permanent factsFabiana works in supply chain.
Past SimpleAções completas no passado / Completed past actionsHer team handled a delay last quarter.
Present PerfectExperiência acumulada, conexão passado-presente / Accumulated experienceWe have dealt with similar situations.
Key difference / Diferença-chavePast simple = QUANDO importa ("last quarter"). Present perfect = a EXPERIÊNCIA importa ("before", "for years").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"The team handles all vendor contracts."
"We need to check the requirements before signing."
"The was delayed due to customs clearance."
"All key have been informed about the delay."
"We can our vendor relationships to negotiate better terms."
"The demand for Q4 looks promising."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?He mentions the backup vendor option.
?Marcus calls about a shipment delay.
?They agree on next steps and a follow-up call.
?Fabiana suggests a cross-functional meeting.
?He explains the customs clearance issue.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I work in the supply chain department at Bloomberg.
Trabalho no departamento de supply chain da Bloomberg.
Our main vendor is based in Singapore.
Nosso principal fornecedor fica em Singapura.
We have dealt with similar situations before.
Já lidamos com situações parecidas antes.
The procurement team handles all vendor contracts.
A equipe de aquisição cuida de todos os contratos com fornecedores.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

A new international vendor asks you to describe your role. What do you say?
A "I do supply chain things at Bloomberg."
B "I am responsible for procurement and vendor management in the supply chain department."
C "I buy stuff for the company."
Your manager asks for a status update on a delayed shipment. How do you respond?
A "The shipment is late. I do not know why."
B "Everything is fine, do not worry."
C "The shipment has been delayed due to customs clearance. I have already contacted the carrier for an updated timeline."
A colleague from another department asks what "compliance" means in your context. You explain:
A "It means ensuring our vendors meet all regulatory and quality standards."
B "It is about being nice to vendors."
C "It means complaining about problems."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe your role at Bloomberg in English. What does your team do? What is the most challenging part of communicating with international partners?

Survival Card -- Lesson 1

1I am responsible for procurement and vendor management.Sou responsável por aquisição e gestão de fornecedores.
2We need to check the compliance requirements.Precisamos verificar os requisitos de conformidade.
3The shipment has been delayed due to customs clearance.A remessa foi atrasada por causa do desembaraço aduaneiro.
4Could we schedule a call to discuss contingency options?Podemos agendar uma ligação para discutir opções de contingência?
5I have already contacted the backup vendor.Já entrei em contato com o fornecedor reserva.
Aula 02 -- Pre-class

First Contact -- Introducing Yourself to International Suppliers

Third conditional in supply chain contexts. New vocabulary: lead time, purchase order, freight, sourcing, invoice, end-to-end, turnaround, carrier, clearance, allocation. Expressions: carry out, look into, put forward.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Lead time -- the time between placing an order and receiving it
"The lead time for this component is four weeks." (Prazo de entrega)
Purchase order -- a formal document requesting goods from a supplier
"We submitted the purchase order yesterday." (Ordem de compra)
Freight -- goods transported by truck, ship, or plane; also the cost of transport
"The freight cost was lower than expected." (Frete)
Sourcing -- the process of finding suppliers for goods or services
"We are sourcing new vendors for the APAC region." (Fornecimento)
Invoice -- a document showing what you owe for goods or services
"Please send the invoice to our finance department." (Fatura)
End-to-end -- covering the entire process from start to finish
"We offer an end-to-end supply chain solution." (Ponta a ponta)
Turnaround -- the time needed to complete a task or process
"They promised a two-week turnaround on repairs." (Tempo de resposta)
Carrier -- a company that transports goods
"Our carrier confirmed the delivery date." (Transportadora)
Clearance -- official permission for goods to pass through customs
"Customs clearance took longer than expected." (Desembaraço)
Allocation -- the distribution of resources or products to different areas
"The allocation of terminals to each office is decided quarterly." (Alocação)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Lead time
Purchase order
Freight
Sourcing
Invoice
End-to-end
Turnaround
Carrier
Clearance
Allocation

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Last month, Bloomberg received a purchase order from a new client in Tokyo. The lead time was tight, and the carrier could not guarantee clearance before the deadline. Fabiana's team had to find an alternative sourcing strategy. "If we had started the procurement process earlier, we would have avoided the delay," Fabiana explained. "And if the vendor had sent the invoice on time, we would not have missed the freight window." In the end, the team managed to carry out a fast turnaround by leveraging their end-to-end contract with a backup supplier. The allocation was adjusted, and the shipment arrived just two days late.

1. What structure does Fabiana use to talk about a past situation that did NOT happen?
A "If we start earlier, we will avoid the delay."
B "If we had started earlier, we would have avoided the delay."
C "If we started earlier, we would avoid the delay."
2. Why does the third conditional use "had + past participle" in the if-clause?
A Because the action is happening now.
B Because it refers to an unreal past situation that cannot be changed.
C Because it describes a future plan.
3. What does "carry out" mean in "carry out a fast turnaround"?
A To execute or complete a task.
B To transport goods outside.
C To cancel a process.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Third Conditional

GRAMMAR

Usado para falar de situações irreais no passado. / Used to talk about unreal past situations.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Affirmative / AfirmativaIf + had + past participle, would have + past participleIf we had started earlier, we would have avoided the delay.
Negative / NegativaIf + had not + past participle, would not have + past participleIf the vendor had not sent the invoice late, we would not have missed the deadline.
Question / PerguntaWould + subject + have + past participle if + had + past participle?Would you have chosen a different carrier if you had known the freight cost?
Key point / Ponto-chaveO third conditional fala de um passado IRREAL. Não aconteceu. Serve para refletir: "O que TERIA acontecido se..."

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"If we had checked the earlier, we would have caught the error."
"The was too short for customs clearance."
"We need to alternative carriers for this route."
"If the had arrived on time, we would have met the deadline."
"She wants to a new sourcing strategy."
"The of terminals was revised after the delay."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?The freight cost is lower than expected.
?Fabiana receives an email from a new vendor in Munich.
?They agree on next steps for the end-to-end contract.
?She reviews the purchase order and checks compliance.
?She schedules a call to discuss lead time and sourcing.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

If we had known about your services earlier, we would have reached out sooner.
Se tivéssemos sabido dos seus serviços antes, teríamos entrado em contato mais cedo.
Could you tell me about your standard lead time?
Poderia me falar sobre o prazo de entrega padrão?
I would like to look into your end-to-end contract options.
Gostaria de explorar as opções de contrato ponta a ponta.
Let me put forward a proposal for our next steps.
Deixe-me apresentar uma proposta para os próximos passos.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

You are meeting a new international vendor for the first time on a video call. How do you introduce yourself?
A "Hi, I do supply chain stuff."
B "Good morning. I am Fabiana Silva from Bloomberg's supply chain department. I handle procurement and vendor management for the APAC region."
C "Hello. Bloomberg sent me to talk to you."
The vendor asks about a past delay. You want to express regret about not acting sooner. You say:
A "If we contact you earlier, we avoid the problem."
B "If we had contacted you earlier, we would have avoided the delay."
C "If we would have contacted you earlier, we avoid the delay."
You want to suggest exploring a new sourcing option. The best way to say it is:
A "We should look at things."
B "I want to carry on with the old vendor."
C "I would like to look into alternative sourcing options for this region."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are on a video call with a new international supplier. Introduce yourself and your role at Bloomberg. Then describe a past situation where, if something had been different, the outcome would have been better. Use at least one third conditional sentence.

Survival Card -- Lesson 2

1I am responsible for procurement and vendor management at Bloomberg.Sou responsável por aquisição e gestão de fornecedores na Bloomberg.
2Could you tell me about your standard lead time?Poderia me falar sobre o prazo de entrega padrão?
3If we had known about your services earlier, we would have reached out sooner.Se tivéssemos sabido dos seus serviços antes, teríamos entrado em contato mais cedo.
4I would like to look into your end-to-end contract options.Gostaria de explorar as opções de contrato ponta a ponta.
5Let me put forward a proposal for our next steps.Deixe-me apresentar uma proposta para os próximos passos.
Aula 03 -- Pre-class

When Things Go Wrong -- Handling Delays and Escalations

Modal perfects in supply chain crisis contexts. New vocabulary: escalation, bottleneck, backlog, disruption, contingency, expedite, deadline, inventory, penalty, oversight. Expressions: bring about, account for, phase out.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Escalation -- raising an issue to a higher level of authority
"The escalation reached the VP within hours." (Escalonamento)
Bottleneck -- a narrow point that slows down the entire process
"The bottleneck is in the documentation process." (Gargalo)
Backlog -- an accumulation of unfinished work or orders
"We have a backlog of thirty units at the port." (Acumulado)
Disruption -- a major interruption to the normal flow of operations
"The disruption affected all APAC deliveries." (Ruptura)
Contingency -- a backup plan in case the main plan fails
"We need a contingency plan for this route." (Contingência)
Expedite -- to speed up a process or make it happen faster
"Can we expedite the customs clearance?" (Agilizar)
Deadline -- the final date by which something must be completed
"The deadline for clearance is Thursday." (Prazo final)
Inventory -- the stock of goods available for sale or distribution
"Inventory is running low at the Tokyo office." (Estoque)
Penalty -- a financial punishment for breaking rules or missing deadlines
"We will face a penalty from the client." (Penalidade)
Oversight -- a mistake caused by not paying enough attention
"The oversight in compliance caused the delay." (Descuido)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Escalation
Bottleneck
Backlog
Disruption
Contingency
Expedite
Deadline
Inventory
Penalty
Oversight

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Last week, a major disruption hit the APAC terminal shipment. The carrier should have confirmed the customs clearance on Friday, but they did not. As a result, a backlog of thirty units is now sitting at the port. The bottleneck was caused by an oversight in the documentation process — the logistics team must have missed the updated compliance requirements. Fabiana believes the escalation might have been avoided if they had implemented a contingency plan earlier. Now the team is working to expedite the process before the deadline on Thursday, or they will face a penalty from the client. The inventory at the Tokyo office is running critically low.

1. What structure does the text use to criticize the carrier for NOT confirming?
A "should have confirmed" -- the right thing that did NOT happen
B "must have confirmed" -- a strong deduction
C "might have confirmed" -- a possibility
2. Why does the text say "must have missed" about the logistics team?
A Because they are criticizing the team.
B Because they are making a strong deduction about what probably happened.
C Because they are not sure at all.
3. What does "might have been avoided" express?
A Certainty that it was avoided.
B Criticism of someone who did not avoid it.
C A possibility -- maybe it could have been avoided, maybe not.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Modal Perfects

GRAMMAR

Modal + have + past participle = falar sobre o passado com diferentes significados. / Modal + have + past participle = talk about the past with different meanings.

Modal PerfectMeaning / SignificadoExample / Exemplo
should have + ppO certo que NÃO aconteceu (crítica/arrependimento) / The right thing that did NOT happenWe should have flagged this earlier.
must have + ppDedução forte sobre o passado / Strong deduction about the pastThey must have missed the deadline.
might have + ppPossibilidade (talvez tenha acontecido) / Possibility about the pastThe disruption might have been avoided.
Ponto-chave / Key pointTodos usam modal + HAVE + past participle. A diferença está no SIGNIFICADO: should = crítica, must = certeza, might = possibilidade.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"The carrier confirmed the clearance last Friday."
"They submitted the old forms by mistake."
"The might have been avoided with better planning."
"We need to the customs clearance before the deadline."
"The was in the documentation process."
"If we miss the , we will face a penalty."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?A backlog of thirty units is sitting at the port.
?Marcus reports a serious shipment disruption.
?The team implements a contingency plan.
?Fabiana escalates the issue and expedites the process.
?The carrier missed the customs clearance deadline.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

The carrier should have confirmed the clearance last week.
A transportadora deveria ter confirmado o desembaraço na semana passada.
They must have submitted the old forms by mistake.
Eles devem ter enviado os formulários antigos por engano.
The disruption might have been avoided with a contingency plan.
A ruptura poderia ter sido evitada com um plano de contingência.
Can we expedite the customs clearance before the deadline?
Podemos agilizar o desembaraço aduaneiro antes do prazo final?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

A shipment is two weeks late and you are almost certain the carrier missed the customs forms. You say:
A "The carrier should have missed the customs forms."
B "The carrier must have missed the customs forms."
C "The carrier will miss the customs forms."
Your manager asks why the team did not activate the contingency plan. You express criticism:
A "We should have activated the contingency plan immediately."
B "We must have activated the contingency plan immediately."
C "We might have activated the contingency plan immediately."
You are not sure, but you think the penalty clause may have been waived. You say:
A "The penalty must have been waived."
B "The penalty might have been waived."
C "The penalty should have been waived."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about a real or fictional supply chain problem. Describe what happened, what should have been done differently, and what you would recommend as a contingency plan. Use at least two modal perfects.

Survival Card -- Lesson 3

1The carrier should have confirmed the clearance last week.A transportadora deveria ter confirmado o desembaraço na semana passada.
2They must have submitted the old forms by mistake.Eles devem ter enviado os formulários antigos por engano.
3The disruption might have been avoided with automated alerts.A ruptura poderia ter sido evitada com alertas automáticos.
4Can we expedite the customs clearance before the deadline?Podemos agilizar o desembaraço aduaneiro antes do prazo?
5We need to account for the delay and activate the contingency plan.Precisamos justificar o atraso e ativar o plano de contingência.
Aula 04 -- Pre-class

Navigating Hierarchy -- Communicating Across Levels

WISH + past simple / past perfect (I wish I knew..., I wish I had known...) for expressing regret. New vocabulary: hierarchy, counterpart, align, bandwidth, chain of command, peer, subordinate, delegate, oversight, rapport, liaison, mandate, buy-in. Expressions: report to, loop in, touch base.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Hierarchy -- the levels of authority in an organization
"Understanding the hierarchy tells you who decides what." (Hierarquia)
Counterpart -- a person with the same role in another team
"Klaus is my counterpart on the supplier side." (Contraparte)
Align -- to bring people or plans into agreement
"We need to align with the director before we act." (Alinhar)
Bandwidth -- the time and energy you have for more work
"I don't have the bandwidth to lead another project." (Disponibilidade)
Chain of command -- the order of authority decisions flow through
"Follow the chain of command before you escalate." (Cadeia de comando)
Peer -- a colleague at the same level as you
"I discussed the issue with a peer in logistics." (Colega de mesmo nível)
Subordinate -- a person who works under your authority
"A good manager supports every subordinate." (Subordinado)
Delegate -- to give a task or responsibility to someone
"I will delegate the report to my analyst." (Delegar)
Oversight -- responsibility for supervising work
"The director has oversight of all APAC accounts." (Supervisão)
Rapport -- a relationship of trust and easy communication
"She built strong rapport with the whole team." (Sintonia)
Liaison -- a person who connects two teams
"Maria is our liaison with their logistics team." (Elo de ligação)
Mandate -- the official authority to decide or act
"He has no mandate to approve large discounts." (Mandato)
Buy-in -- agreement and support from the people involved
"We need buy-in from senior management first." (Adesão)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Hierarchy
Counterpart
Align
Bandwidth
Chain of command
Peer
Subordinate
Delegate
Oversight
Rapport

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

After the Munich delay, Fabiana decided to map TechFlow's hierarchy. Klaus, her counterpart, explained that the operations director holds full oversight and the final mandate. Looking back, Fabiana said: "I wish I had known the chain of command from the start." She also admitted: "I wish I knew the director personally." To move faster, she chose to align with Klaus first, build rapport with his peer Anna, and ask Klaus to delegate routine tasks to his subordinates. Because Klaus needs buy-in from the director for any credit, Maria will act as the liaison and Fabiana will free up some bandwidth to lead the next review.

1. Which sentence expresses regret about a PAST situation?
A "I wish I know the chain of command."
B "I wish I had known the chain of command from the start."
C "I wish I will know the chain of command."
2. What does "I wish I knew the director personally" mean?
A She does not know the director now, and regrets it.
B She knew the director in the past.
C She will meet the director tomorrow.
3. Why does Klaus need buy-in from the director?
A Because the director has oversight and the final mandate for credits.
B Because the director is his subordinate.
C Because Anna is the liaison for freight.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- WISH

GRAMMAR

Usado para expressar arrependimento ou desejo de mudança. / Used to express regret or a desire for change.

Form / FormaMeaning / SentidoExample / Exemplo
wish + past simpleArrependimento sobre o presente / Regret about nowI wish I knew who my counterpart is.
wish + past perfectArrependimento sobre o passado / Regret about the pastI wish I had aligned with the director sooner.
wish + wouldIncomodo com o comportamento de alguém / Annoyance with behaviorI wish they would respect the chain of command.
Key point / Ponto-chaveApós WISH, recue um tempo verbal. Nunca use "I wish I know" nem "I wish I would have known".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Understanding the tells you who decides what."
"Klaus is my on the supplier side."
"I will the routine follow-ups to my analyst."
"The director has full of the APAC accounts."
"It takes time to build with senior management."
"We need from senior management before we proceed."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?Fabiana aligns with her counterpart on the next steps.
?Fabiana realizes she does not know TechFlow's hierarchy.
?They secure buy-in from senior management for the solution.
?Klaus explains the chain of command and introduces his director.
?The team builds rapport with their peers on the supply side.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I wish I had aligned with my counterpart earlier.
Eu queria ter alinhado com minha contraparte antes.
Could you help me understand the chain of command?
Você poderia me ajudar a entender a cadeia de comando?
I will delegate this and keep you in the loop.
Vou delegar isso e manter você informado.
Building rapport with senior management takes time.
Construir sintonia com a alta gestão leva tempo.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

You do not know who the decision-maker is at the supplier, and you regret it now. How do you express that?
A "I wish I know who the decision-maker is."
B "I wish I knew who the decision-maker is."
C "I wish I would know who the decision-maker is."
You forgot to align with your counterpart before a meeting, and it caused problems. How do you reflect on it?
A "I wish I had aligned with my counterpart before the meeting."
B "I wish I would have aligned with my counterpart."
C "I wish I align with my counterpart."
You want to escalate an issue the right way, respecting the hierarchy. The best approach is:
A "I will email the CEO directly and skip everyone else."
B "I will just wait and hope someone notices."
C "I will align with my counterpart first, then ask him to loop in the director for buy-in."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about the hierarchy at Bloomberg or at one of your suppliers. Describe who your counterpart is, who has oversight, and how you build rapport. Then reflect with at least two WISH structures (for example: "I wish I knew..." and "I wish I had...").

Survival Card -- Lesson 4

1Who is my counterpart on your side?Quem é minha contraparte do seu lado?
2I want to align with you before I escalate.Quero alinhar com você antes de escalar.
3Could you loop in the person with oversight?Você poderia incluir a pessoa responsável pela supervisão?
4We need buy-in from senior management first.Precisamos da adesão da alta gestão primeiro.
5I wish I had known the hierarchy earlier.Eu queria ter conhecido a hierarquia antes.
Aula 05 -- Pre-class

The Vendor Call -- Running International Supply Chain Meetings

Advanced passive voice (it was shipped, it must be renegotiated, it is being applied) + causative have/get something done (we had the contract reviewed, I will get the quote revised). New vocabulary: agenda, quote, renegotiate, SLA, incoterms, customs, tariff, reroute, fulfillment, surcharge, expedite, consignment, action item.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Agenda -- the list of topics planned for a meeting
"Let us follow the agenda so nothing is missed." (Pauta)
Quote -- a formal statement of the price for goods or services
"The supplier sent a quote for the new order." (Orçamento)
Renegotiate -- to discuss an agreement again to change its terms
"We need to renegotiate the contract this quarter." (Renegociar)
SLA -- a contract defining the standard of service promised
"The SLA sets a delivery time of five days." (Acordo de nível de serviço)
Incoterms -- rules that define who pays and who is responsible in shipping
"Under these incoterms, the seller pays the freight." (Termos de comércio internacional)
Customs -- the authority that controls goods crossing a border
"The shipment is still being held at customs." (Alfândega)
Tariff -- a government tax on imported goods
"A new tariff raised the cost of every unit." (Tarifa)
Reroute -- to send a shipment along a different path
"They had to reroute the cargo through another port." (Redirecionar)
Fulfillment -- the whole process of getting an order to the customer
"Faster fulfillment keeps our customers happy." (Atendimento de pedidos)
Surcharge -- an extra charge added on top of the normal price
"A fuel surcharge was added to the freight." (Sobretaxa)
Expedite -- to make a process or shipment happen faster
"We asked the vendor to expedite the order." (Agilizar)
Consignment -- a batch of goods sent together to a customer
"The first consignment arrives next Monday." (Remessa)
Action item -- a specific task assigned to a named owner in a meeting
"Each action item has an owner and a deadline." (Item de ação)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Agenda
Quote
Renegotiate
SLA
Customs
Tariff
Reroute
Surcharge
Expedite
Consignment

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Before the vendor call, Klaus sent the agenda. Three points had to be covered. First, the SLA must be renegotiated, because delivery targets keep being missed. Second, the latest consignment was rerouted through Rotterdam, and a surcharge was added to the freight. Third, a new tariff is being applied at customs, so the quote must be revised. To prepare, Fabiana had the numbers checked by finance and got the contract reviewed by legal. On the call, the goods were expedited, every action item was assigned an owner, and the order was fulfilled on time.

1. Which sentence is in the passive voice?
A "Klaus sent the agenda."
B "The consignment was rerouted through Rotterdam."
C "Klaus reviews the contract."
2. What does "Fabiana had the numbers checked by finance" mean?
A She arranged for the finance team to check them.
B She checked the numbers herself.
C The numbers were never checked.
3. Why must the quote be revised?
A Because a new tariff is being applied at customs.
B Because the agenda was too long.
C Because the SLA was cancelled.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Passive & Causative

GRAMMAR

Usado para focar na ação ou para organizar que outra pessoa faça algo. / Used to focus on the action or to arrange for someone else to do something.

Form / FormaMeaning / SentidoExample / Exemplo
be + past participlePassiva: foco na ação / Passive: focus on the actionThe order was fulfilled on time.
modal + be + p.p.Passiva modal: obrigação, regra / Modal passive: rule, obligationThe SLA must be renegotiated.
have + object + p.p.Causativo: organizar que outro faça / Causative: arrange for someone elseWe had the contract reviewed by legal.
get + object + p.p.Causativo informal: conseguir fazer / Informal causative: manage toI will get the quote revised by Friday.

Key point / Ponto-chave: no causativo, a ordem é sempre have/get + a COISA + past participle. Nunca "we had reviewed the contract by legal" (isso é passado perfeito, sentido diferente).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Let us follow the so nothing is missed."
"The must be renegotiated before we sign."
"A fuel was added to the freight."
"A new is being applied at customs."
"We asked the vendor to the order."
"Each has an owner and a deadline."

Stage 2: Put the Call in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?The delayed consignment is rerouted and expedited through customs.
?Klaus sends Fabiana the agenda for the vendor call.
?Every action item is assigned, so the order is fulfilled on time.
?The SLA is renegotiated and stricter delivery targets are agreed.
?A revised quote is prepared after a new tariff is applied.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

The SLA must be renegotiated before we sign.
O acordo de nível de serviço precisa ser renegociado antes de assinarmos.
Can the consignment be expedited through customs?
A remessa pode ser agilizada na alfândega?
We are having the contract reviewed by legal.
Estamos mandando revisar o contrato pelo jurídico.
Every action item will be assigned an owner today.
Cada item de ação terá um responsável hoje.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

You want to report a late delivery, focusing on the action and not on who did it. How do you say it?
A "Someone fulfilled the order late."
B "The order was fulfilled two days late."
C "The order fulfilled late."
You arranged for the legal team to review the contract. How do you describe it with the causative?
A "We had the contract reviewed by legal."
B "We had reviewed the contract by legal."
C "We have review the contract by legal."
The delivery targets are unrealistic and the agreement needs new terms. The best thing to say on the call is:
A "We will just accept the missed targets."
B "The SLA must be renegotiated, and a revised quote should be sent."
C "Nothing needs to be changed about the contract."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine your next vendor call at Bloomberg. Describe the agenda, one thing that must be renegotiated, and one thing that is being expedited. Then say two things you are having done by other teams, using the causative ("we had ... done" / "I will get ... done").

Survival Card -- Lesson 5

1Let us follow the agenda so nothing is missed.Vamos seguir a pauta para não esquecer nada.
2The SLA must be renegotiated before we sign.O acordo de nível de serviço precisa ser renegociado antes de assinarmos.
3Can the consignment be expedited through customs?A remessa pode ser agilizada na alfândega?
4We are having the contract reviewed by legal.Estamos mandando revisar o contrato pelo jurídico.
5Every action item will be assigned an owner today.Cada item de ação terá um responsável hoje.
Aula 06 -- Pre-class

Making Your Case -- Persuasive Language in Negotiations

Cleft sentences for emphasis in supply chain negotiations. New vocabulary: proposal, viable, framework, threshold, commitment, concession, margin, clause, trade-off, counter-offer, stakeholder buy-in, leverage. Expressions: make a compelling case, reach common ground, sweeten the deal.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Proposal -- a formal plan presented for discussion
"I would like to put forward a proposal for the Q4 contract." (Proposta)
Viable -- realistic and capable of working successfully
"This deal is only viable if we commit to minimum volumes." (Viável)
Framework -- a basic structure or set of rules for an agreement
"We need a framework for the twelve-month contract." (Estrutura)
Threshold -- the minimum level that triggers an action
"If volumes fall below the threshold, the discount is void." (Limite mínimo)
Commitment -- a firm promise to do something
"We need a volume commitment before signing." (Compromisso)
Concession -- something you give up to reach an agreement
"We made a concession on price in exchange for faster delivery." (Concessão)
Margin -- the difference between cost and selling price
"Our margin is too thin to offer a bigger discount." (Margem)
Clause -- a specific condition in a contract
"The penalty clause protects us against late delivery." (Cláusula)
Trade-off -- gaining one thing means losing another
"The trade-off is lower price but longer lead time." (Troca)
Counter-offer -- a response to an offer with different terms
"They rejected our price but sent a counter-offer." (Contraproposta)
Stakeholder buy-in -- agreement and support from decision-makers
"We need stakeholder buy-in before finalizing." (Adesão dos interessados)
Leverage -- to use something to maximum advantage
"We can leverage our volume to negotiate better terms." (Alavancar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Proposal
Viable
Framework
Threshold
Commitment
Concession
Margin
Clause
Trade-off
Counter-offer
Stakeholder buy-in
Leverage

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Last week, Fabiana negotiated a new framework agreement with TechFlow Solutions. What she focused on was building a case around volume commitment rather than price alone. It was the freight concession that really got Klaus's attention, not the discount on unit price. The reason this matters is that it gives Bloomberg predictability for supply chain planning. The trade-off was accepting a longer lead time in exchange for a lower threshold. Fabiana also negotiated a revised penalty clause as a key concession. What she recommended next was preparing a formal proposal with exact volume numbers to secure stakeholder buy-in.

1. What structure does the text use to emphasize what Fabiana focused on?
A WH-cleft: "What she focused on was..."
B Passive: "It was focused on by her..."
C Modal perfect: "She should have focused on..."
2. What structure emphasizes the freight concession as the key factor?
A "The freight concession got their attention."
B IT-cleft: "It was the freight concession that got their attention."
C "The freight concession should have got their attention."
3. Why does the text use "The reason this matters is that..."?
A To express regret about the past.
B To emphasize WHY something is important -- giving a clear justification.
C To make a deduction about what happened.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Cleft Sentences

GRAMMAR

Cleft sentences DIVIDEM a frase para dar ênfase. / Cleft sentences SPLIT the sentence to add emphasis.

Type / TipoStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
IT-cleftIt is/was + [foco] + that/who...It is the margin that concerns us.
WH-cleftWhat + [oração] + is/was...What we need is stakeholder buy-in.
Negative IT-cleftIt is not X that..., it is YIt is not the price, it is the timeline.
Ponto-chave / Key pointCleft sentences são poderosas em negociações porque direcionam a atenção para o que REALMENTE importa.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"It is the that makes the deal viable."
"What we need is a revised for the contract."
"The is lower price but longer lead time."
"They sent a with a lower threshold."
"We need before finalizing the deal."
"The penalty protects us against late delivery."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?Klaus responds with a freight reduction offer.
?Fabiana opens the negotiation with TechFlow.
?Fabiana debriefs the team and recommends a formal proposal.
?They discuss a twelve-month framework agreement.
?She proposes a revised pricing structure based on volume.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

It is the volume commitment that makes this deal viable.
É o compromisso de volume que torna esse negócio viável.
What we need is stakeholder buy-in before the board meeting.
O que precisamos é adesão dos interessados antes da reunião do conselho.
The reason I pushed back is that the penalty clause was too aggressive.
O motivo pelo qual recuei é que a cláusula de penalidade era muito agressiva.
What I suggest is that we lock in the terms for twelve months.
O que sugiro é que fixemos os termos por doze meses.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

You want to emphasize that the timeline, not the price, is your main concern. You say:
A "The timeline is more important than the price."
B "It is not the price that concerns me, it is the timeline."
C "I should have focused on the timeline, not the price."
You want to tell your team what the next priority is. The most persuasive way is:
A "What we need to do next is prepare the formal proposal."
B "We need to prepare the formal proposal."
C "The proposal must have been prepared."
Your manager asks why you made a concession. The best structure is:
A "I made a concession because I wanted the deal."
B "The reason I made a concession is that the vendor's counter-offer included priority allocation."
C "I should have made the concession earlier."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are negotiating a new contract with a supplier. Make your case using at least two cleft sentences. What is the most important term for you? What trade-off would you accept? Why?

Survival Card -- Lesson 6

1It is the volume commitment that makes this deal viable.É o compromisso de volume que torna esse negócio viável.
2What we need is stakeholder buy-in before the board meeting.O que precisamos é adesão dos interessados antes da reunião.
3It is not the price that concerns me, it is the timeline.Não é o preço que me preocupa, é o prazo.
4The reason I pushed back is that the penalty clause was too aggressive.O motivo pelo qual recuei é que a cláusula de penalidade era muito agressiva.
5What I suggest is that we lock in the terms for twelve months.O que sugiro é que fixemos os termos por doze meses.
Aula 07 -- Pre-class

Saying the Unsaid -- Emphasis and Focus in Meetings

Fronting and inversion for emphasis. New vocabulary: scrutiny, transparency, credibility, disconnect, withhold, deflect, acknowledge, scope, maturity, stance, elephant in the room, impact.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Scrutiny -- close, critical examination of something
"Our team has never faced this level of scrutiny." (Escrutínio)
Transparency -- openness and honesty in communication
"We need radical transparency going forward." (Transparência)
Credibility -- the trust and respect others have for you
"We lost credibility with the Tokyo office." (Credibilidade)
Disconnect -- a gap between things that should be connected
"There was a disconnect between what was promised and delivered." (Desconexão)
Withhold -- to keep back or hide information
"At no point should anyone withhold information." (Reter)
Deflect -- to redirect blame or attention away
"Nobody tried to deflect blame during the meeting." (Desviar)
Acknowledge -- to recognize or admit something is true
"Only after the complaint did we acknowledge the problem." (Reconhecer)
Scope -- the range or extent of something
"Had I known the full scope, I would have acted sooner." (Escopo)
Maturity -- the level of responsibility in an organization
"Not deflecting blame is a sign of maturity." (Maturidade)
Stance -- a publicly stated position on an issue
"Seldom does leadership take such a strong stance." (Posicionamento)
Elephant in the room -- the obvious problem nobody wants to mention
"Let me address the elephant in the room." (O elefante na sala)
Impact -- the effect or influence of an event
"Had we communicated earlier, the impact would have been minimal." (Impacto)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Scrutiny
Transparency
Credibility
Disconnect
Withhold
Deflect
Acknowledge
Scope
Maturity
Stance
Elephant in the room
Impact

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

The cross-functional meeting was intense. Rarely had the team faced this level of scrutiny. Not only did they miss the Q3 deadline, but they also lost credibility with the Tokyo office. There was a clear disconnect between what was promised and what was delivered. Marcus took a firm stance on transparency: "Under no circumstances should anyone withhold information about delivery risks." Only after the client complained did the team acknowledge the problem. Little did they realize the full scope of the impact. Nobody tried to deflect blame, which showed real maturity. Finally, someone addressed the elephant in the room: the system itself needed to change.

1. What structure does "Rarely had the team faced..." use?
A Fronting with inversion: negative adverb + auxiliary + subject
B Cleft sentence: It was... that...
C Passive voice: was faced by the team
2. What is the effect of putting "Under no circumstances" at the beginning?
A It makes the sentence informal.
B It adds dramatic emphasis and authority to the rule.
C It makes the sentence conditional.
3. What does "Only after the client complained did the team acknowledge" tell us?
A The team waited too long -- they only acted AFTER the complaint.
B The team acted before the complaint.
C The client did not complain.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Fronting and Inversion

GRAMMAR

Quando uma expressão negativa vai para o INÍCIO da frase, sujeito e verbo INVERTEM. / When a negative expression goes to the START, subject and verb INVERT.

Expression / ExpressãoNormalInverted (Emphatic)
Rarely / SeldomWe rarely see this.Rarely do we see this.
Not only... but alsoWe missed + we lost.Not only did we miss, but also...
Under no circumstancesThis should not happen.Under no circumstances should this happen.
Ponto-chaveA inversão cria ÊNFASE dramática. Use em reuniões, apresentações e momentos de liderança.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

" have I seen such a disconnect between teams."
"Not only did we miss the deadline, we also lost credibility."
"Under no should anyone withhold information."
"Little did we how much this would affect the relationship."
"Only after the client complained did we the problem."
"Never again should we wait for the client to an issue."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?The team discusses what went wrong and why communication broke down.
?Marcus opens the meeting with a direct statement.
?They agree on next steps to rebuild trust.
?Fabiana proposes a new transparency framework.
?He addresses the Q3 failure and loss of credibility.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Rarely have I seen such a disconnect between teams.
Raramente vi tamanha desconexão entre equipes.
Under no circumstances should anyone withhold information.
Sob nenhuma circunstância alguém deve reter informações.
Not only did we miss the deadline, but we also lost credibility.
Não só perdemos o prazo, como também perdemos credibilidade.
Little did we realize how much this would affect the relationship.
Mal sabíamos o quanto isso afetaria o relacionamento.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

You want to emphasize that your team has NEVER experienced this before. The most emphatic way is:
A "Our team has never faced this."
B "Never before has our team faced this level of scrutiny."
C "It is our team that has never faced this."
You want to set a firm rule about withholding information. The most authoritative way is:
A "Under no circumstances should team members withhold risk information."
B "Team members should not withhold risk information."
C "I wish team members would not withhold information."
You want to say two bad things happened at once. Use inversion:
A "We lost the contract and the vendor trust."
B "Not only did we lose the contract, but we also damaged the vendor relationship."
C "It was the contract and the trust that we lost."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are opening a post-mortem meeting after a major supply chain failure. Address the elephant in the room, describe what went wrong, and set new rules for the team. Use at least three inverted sentences for emphasis.

Survival Card -- Lesson 7

1Rarely have I seen such a disconnect between teams.Raramente vi tamanha desconexão entre equipes.
2Not only did we miss the deadline, but we also lost credibility.Não só perdemos o prazo, como também perdemos credibilidade.
3Under no circumstances should anyone withhold information.Sob nenhuma circunstância alguém deve reter informações.
4Little did we realize how much this would affect the relationship.Mal sabíamos o quanto isso afetaria o relacionamento.
5Never again should we wait for the client to flag an issue.Nunca mais devemos esperar o cliente sinalizar um problema.
Aula 08 -- Pre-class

What Should Have Happened -- Reviewing Supply Chain Failures

Mixed conditionals connecting past and present. New vocabulary: root cause, corrective action, lessons learned, benchmark, defective, inspection, audit, preventive, protocol, rollout, takeaway, flawed.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Root cause -- the fundamental reason behind a problem
"The root cause was a flawed quality control process." (Causa raiz)
Corrective action -- steps taken to fix a problem after it happens
"The corrective action plan was implemented immediately." (Ação corretiva)
Lessons learned -- knowledge gained from analyzing past experiences
"The lessons learned document was shared with all teams." (Lições aprendidas)
Benchmark -- a standard used to measure quality or performance
"We need clearer quality benchmarks for all vendors." (Referência)
Defective -- having a fault that prevents proper function
"The defective units are sitting in our warehouse." (Defeituoso)
Inspection -- a careful examination to check quality
"The defective units should not have passed inspection." (Inspeção)
Audit -- an official examination of records or processes
"We need quarterly vendor audits." (Auditoria)
Preventive -- actions taken to stop a problem before it happens
"The preventive measures include a new checklist." (Preventivo)
Protocol -- a set of official rules or procedures
"We need a revised inspection protocol." (Protocolo)
Rollout -- the process of launching a new system or product
"The APAC terminal rollout had several issues." (Implantação)
Takeaway -- the most important conclusion from a review
"The biggest takeaway is that we need earlier detection." (Conclusão principal)
Flawed -- having a weakness or imperfection
"The vendor's process was fundamentally flawed." (Falho)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Root cause
Corrective action
Lessons learned
Benchmark
Defective
Inspection
Audit
Preventive
Protocol
Rollout
Takeaway
Flawed

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

The APAC terminal rollout failed due to a flawed quality control process. If the vendor had run proper inspections, the defective units would not be sitting in our warehouse right now. The root cause analysis revealed that the protocol was outdated. If our internal audit process were stronger, we would be in a much better position today. The corrective action was effective, but the team agreed that preventive measures are more important. The key takeaway from the lessons learned document is clear: if we had established clearer benchmarks from the start, this situation would not exist today.

1. What type of conditional is "If the vendor had run inspections, the units would not be sitting here now"?
A Third conditional (both clauses about the past)
B Mixed conditional (past condition, present result)
C Second conditional (hypothetical present)
2. What type is "If our audit process were stronger, we would be in a better position"?
A Third conditional
B First conditional
C Second/mixed conditional (present condition, present result with past implication)
3. What is the difference between corrective and preventive actions?
A Corrective = fix AFTER the problem; Preventive = stop BEFORE it happens
B They mean the same thing.
C Corrective = future plans; Preventive = past actions

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Mixed Conditionals

GRAMMAR

Mixed conditionals MISTURAM tempos: passado na condição + presente no resultado (ou vice-versa). / Mixed conditionals MIX tenses: past in the condition + present in the result (or vice versa).

Type / TipoIF clauseResult clause
Past → PresentIf + had + past participlewould/could + base verb (agora)
ExemploIf we had implemented the system, we would not be dealing with errors today.
Present → PastIf + were/waswould have + past participle
Ponto-chaveA palavra "today/now/right now" é a pista de que o resultado é PRESENTE. Se o resultado é sobre o passado, use "would have".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"If the vendor had run proper , the defective units would not be here."
"The was a flawed quality control process."
"We need quarterly vendor with measurable benchmarks."
"The plan was implemented immediately."
"The biggest is that we need earlier detection."
"The APAC terminal had several issues."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?Fabiana drafts the lessons learned document.
?The team conducts a post-mortem of the APAC rollout.
?They set benchmarks for quarterly vendor audits.
?The team agrees on corrective and preventive measures.
?They identify vendor quality control as the root cause.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

If we had implemented the system, we would not be dealing with errors today.
Se tivéssemos implementado o sistema, não estaríamos lidando com erros hoje.
If we were more proactive about audits, we would not have ended up here.
Se fôssemos mais proativos com auditorias, não teríamos chegado aqui.
The root cause was a flawed quality control process at the vendor.
A causa raiz foi um processo de controle de qualidade falho no fornecedor.
We need quarterly vendor audits with measurable benchmarks.
Precisamos de auditorias trimestrais com referências mensuráveis.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

You did not create a backup plan last quarter, and NOW you are scrambling. Which is the mixed conditional?
A "If we had created a backup plan, we would have been prepared." (3rd -- both past)
B "If we had created a backup plan, we would not be scrambling right now." (mixed -- past→present)
C "If we create a backup plan, we will not scramble." (1st -- future)
Your company is generally slow at audits. Because of this, you ended up with a flawed vendor LAST YEAR. Which is the mixed conditional?
A "If we were faster at audits, we would not have ended up with a flawed vendor." (mixed -- present→past)
B "If we had been faster at audits, we would not have ended up with a flawed vendor." (3rd -- both past)
C "If we are faster at audits, we will not end up with flawed vendors." (1st -- future)
Which word means "steps taken to fix a problem AFTER it happens"?
A Preventive action
B Corrective action
C Root cause analysis

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about a real or fictional supply chain failure. Describe the root cause, the corrective action taken, and what preventive measures you would recommend. Use at least two mixed conditionals to connect past decisions to present consequences.

Survival Card -- Lesson 8

1If we had implemented the system, we would not be dealing with errors today.Se tivéssemos implementado o sistema, não estaríamos lidando com erros hoje.
2If we were more proactive about audits, we would not have ended up here.Se fôssemos mais proativos com auditorias, não teríamos chegado aqui.
3The root cause was a flawed quality control process.A causa raiz foi um processo de controle de qualidade falho.
4We need quarterly vendor audits with measurable benchmarks.Precisamos de auditorias trimestrais com referências mensuráveis.
5If we follow through on these recommendations, this would not happen again.Se cumprirmos essas recomendações, isso não aconteceria novamente.
Aula 09 -- Pre-class

Habits, Changes, and Adaptations -- Processes That Evolved

Used to / would / get used to for past habits and adaptation. New vocabulary: streamline, transition, evolve, automate, anticipate, optimize, legacy system, overhaul, adapt, mindset, advocate, phase.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Streamline -- to make a process simpler and more efficient
"We streamlined the vendor selection process." (Simplificar)
Transition -- the process of changing from one system to another
"The transition from manual to digital was challenging." (Transição)
Evolve -- to develop gradually over time
"Our supply chain has evolved significantly." (Evoluir)
Automate -- to use technology to do something without human effort
"We automated the tracking process last year." (Automatizar)
Anticipate -- to expect or predict something before it happens
"Now we anticipate problems instead of reacting." (Antecipar)
Optimize -- to make the best possible use of something
"We optimized the freight routing to reduce costs." (Otimizar)
Legacy system -- an old system or technology still in use
"We need to replace the legacy tracking system." (Sistema legado)
Overhaul -- a complete renovation of a system
"The procurement process needs an overhaul." (Revisão completa)
Adapt -- to change behavior to fit a new situation
"We had to adapt quickly to the new requirements." (Adaptar)
Mindset -- a person's way of thinking or attitude
"The shift to a proactive mindset took time." (Mentalidade)
Advocate -- a person who supports or recommends something
"Marcus became the biggest advocate for the new system." (Defensor)
Phase -- a distinct stage in a process of change
"The first phase of the digital transition is complete." (Fase)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Streamline
Transition
Evolve
Automate
Anticipate
Optimize
Legacy system
Overhaul
Adapt
Mindset
Advocate
Phase

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Bloomberg's supply chain has evolved dramatically. Three years ago, the team used to rely entirely on manual tracking spreadsheets. Every Monday, someone would compile the data by hand and email it to the team. The transition to an automated dashboard was challenging. Many people struggled to get used to the new interface. But once they adapted, they became advocates for the new system. The legacy system was eventually phased out completely. The overhaul also changed the mindset: the team used to wait for problems to happen. Now they anticipate them. The entire process has been streamlined and optimized. Today, everyone is used to working with the real-time tools.

1. What structure does "used to rely" use, and what does it mean?
A Past habit that no longer exists -- they relied before but not anymore.
B A repeated future action.
C An ongoing present habit.
2. Why does the text use "would compile" instead of "used to compile"?
A They mean different things.
B Both work for repeated past ACTIONS. "Would" adds a storytelling, nostalgic tone.
C "Would" is more formal than "used to".
3. What is the difference between "get used to" and "be used to"?
A They mean exactly the same thing.
B "Get used to" = the PROCESS of adjusting. "Be used to" = ALREADY adjusted.
C "Get used to" is past, "be used to" is future.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Used to / Would / Get used to

GRAMMAR

Três estruturas para falar de hábitos passados e adaptação. / Three structures for past habits and adaptation.

Structure / EstruturaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
used to + verbHábito ou estado passado (não mais verdade) / Past habit or stateWe used to rely on spreadsheets.
would + verbAção repetida no passado (só ações, não estados) / Repeated past action onlySomeone would compile the data every Monday.
get used to + -ingProcesso de se acostumar / Adjusting to something newI struggled to get used to the new system.
Ponto-chavewould só para AÇÕES repetidas (compile, send, check). used to para ações E estados (be, have, rely).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"We rely on manual spreadsheets."
"Every Friday, Marcus send a weekly summary."
"It took months to the new platform."
"Now I am working with the automated dashboard."
"The entire process has been and optimized."
"We need to replace the with something modern."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?They transitioned to an automated real-time dashboard.
?Bloomberg used to rely on manual tracking spreadsheets.
?The shift from reactive to proactive management is complete.
?People struggled to get used to the new system at first.
?The team would compile data by hand every Monday.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

We used to rely on manual spreadsheets for tracking.
Costávamos depender de planilhas manuais para rastreamento.
Every Monday, someone would compile the data by hand.
Toda segunda, alguém compilava os dados manualmente.
It took time to get used to the new automated system.
Levou tempo para se acostumar com o novo sistema automatizado.
Our supply chain has evolved from reactive to proactive.
Nosso supply chain evoluiu de reativo para proativo.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

A new colleague asks how tracking worked before. The best way to describe the old habit is:
A "We would be using spreadsheets." (wrong use of would)
B "We used to track everything on spreadsheets. Someone would update them every Monday."
C "We are used to tracking on spreadsheets." (means currently adjusted)
Your manager asks if you are comfortable with the new system. You want to say you have adjusted. You say:
A "I used to the new system." (incorrect structure)
B "Yes, I am used to working with it now. It took time to get used to it, but the transition is complete."
C "I would work with the new system." (wrong meaning)
You are describing how the team's mindset changed. The most natural way is:
A "We used to think of change as disruptive. Now we see it as a competitive advantage."
B "We would think of change as disruptive." (would cannot be used with states like "think of")
C "We are getting used to think of change." (wrong: get used to + -ing)

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe how a process at Bloomberg has evolved. What did you used to do? What would you do regularly? What was hardest to get used to? What are you used to now? Use all three structures.

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1We used to rely on manual spreadsheets for tracking.Costávamos depender de planilhas manuais para rastreamento.
2Every Monday, someone would compile the data by hand.Toda segunda, alguém compilava os dados manualmente.
3It took time to get used to the new automated system.Levou tempo para se acostumar com o novo sistema automatizado.
4Now I am used to working with the real-time dashboard.Agora estou acostumada a trabalhar com o painel em tempo real.
5Our supply chain has evolved from reactive to proactive.Nosso supply chain evoluiu de reativo para proativo.
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Projecting Forward -- Timelines, Forecasts, and Strategic Plans

Advanced future forms (will, going to, will have + pp, be about to, be due to, present continuous). New vocabulary: projection, roadmap, scalability, budget allocation, procurement cycle, pilot program, on track, bring forward, accelerate, forecast, milestone, deployment.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Projection -- an estimate of future outcomes based on data
"Based on our projections, demand will grow by twelve percent." (Projeção)
Roadmap -- a visual plan showing milestones and deadlines
"The roadmap shows three key milestones for Q1." (Roteiro)
Scalability -- the ability to grow and handle more demand
"Our strategic priority is scalability across APAC." (Escalabilidade)
Budget allocation -- distributing financial resources to different areas
"We are about to finalize the budget allocation." (Alocação de orçamento)
Procurement cycle -- the complete process from needs to orders
"We may need to accelerate the procurement cycle." (Ciclo de aquisição)
Pilot program -- a small-scale test before full launch
"The pilot program is due to launch in February." (Programa piloto)
On track -- progressing as planned
"We are on track to meet the Q1 milestones." (No caminho certo)
Bring forward -- to move something to an earlier date
"I recommend we bring forward the testing phase." (Antecipar)
Accelerate -- to make something happen faster
"If demand grows, we may need to accelerate." (Acelerar)
Forecast (verb) -- to predict future trends based on data
"The demand forecast indicates a twelve percent increase." (Prever)
Milestone -- a significant checkpoint in a project timeline
"Vendor onboarding is the first milestone." (Marco)
Deployment -- putting a system into active use
"Full deployment is scheduled for mid-March." (Implantação)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Projection
Roadmap
Scalability
Budget allocation
Procurement cycle
Pilot program
On track
Bring forward
Accelerate
Forecast
Milestone
Deployment

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

The strategic plan for Q1 is taking shape. Based on current projections, the team is going to expand the APAC vendor network to five partners. The roadmap shows three key milestones. By the end of Q1, they will have completed the migration to the new platform. The pilot program is due to launch in February, and full deployment is scheduled for mid-March. The budget allocation is about to be finalized. However, the team may need to accelerate the procurement cycle if demand grows faster than forecasted. Marcus will share the revised numbers by Friday. The team is meeting the VP tomorrow to present the plan. Their strategic priority is scalability -- they want to bring forward the testing phase to stay on track.

1. What future form is used for the planned expansion? ("is going to expand")
A Going to -- for a planned decision based on current evidence.
B Will -- for a spontaneous decision.
C Present continuous -- for a fixed arrangement.
2. What does "will have completed" emphasize?
A The action is happening right now.
B The action will be FINISHED before the end of Q1.
C The action was completed in the past.
3. Why does the text use "is meeting" for the VP appointment?
A Present continuous for a fixed arrangement with specific time and place.
B It means the meeting is happening right now.
C It is a general plan without a specific date.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Advanced Future Forms

GRAMMAR

Seis formas de falar sobre o futuro com precisão. / Six ways to talk about the future with precision.

Form / FormaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
going toDecisão planejada / Planned decisionWe are going to expand.
willPromessa / decisão espontânea / PromiseI will finalize by Friday.
will have + ppCompleto ANTES de um ponto futuro / Done beforeWe will have completed by Q1.
be about toFuturo imediato (muito em breve) / Very soonWe are about to finalize.
be due toAgendado / Expected / ScheduledThe pilot is due to launch.
Ponto-chaveCada forma tem uma NUANCE diferente. "By" = pista para future perfect. "Tomorrow at 10" = present continuous.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Based on our , demand will grow by twelve percent."
"The shows three key milestones for Q1."
"By Q1, we will have completed the ."
"I recommend we the testing phase."
"We are to meet the Q1 milestones."
"Our strategic priority is across APAC."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?Fabiana updates the roadmap with revised milestones.
?Marcus presents the strategic plan for Q1.
?The team agrees to accelerate testing to meet the March deadline.
?They identify a risk with the deployment timeline.
?The team reviews demand projections and vendor targets.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Based on our projections, we are going to expand the vendor network.
Com base em nossas projeções, vamos expandir a rede de fornecedores.
By the end of Q1, we will have completed the migration.
Até o final do Q1, teremos completado a migração.
The pilot program is due to launch in February.
O programa piloto está previsto para lançar em fevereiro.
I recommend we bring forward the testing phase to close the gap.
Recomendo que antecipemos a fase de testes para fechar a lacuna.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each professional situation.

You have a clear plan to expand vendors next quarter. The best future form is:
A "We are going to expand the vendor network." (planned decision)
B "We will expand the vendor network." (sounds like spontaneous decision)
C "We are expanding the vendor network." (needs specific time for arrangement)
Your manager asks when the migration will be done. You say it will be FINISHED before end of March. Use:
A "By March, we will complete the migration." (missing completion emphasis)
B "By the end of March, we will have completed the migration." (future perfect)
C "We are about to complete the migration by March." (about to = very soon, not months away)
The budget is almost done -- just a few more signatures needed, probably today. You say:
A "We are about to finalize the budget allocation." (immediate future)
B "We are going to finalize the budget allocation." (sounds like a plan, not imminent)
C "The budget is due to be finalized." (sounds like a scheduled event, not happening right now)

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Present a strategic plan for a Bloomberg initiative. Include a roadmap with at least 3 milestones, a forecast, one risk, and a recommendation. Use as many different future forms as possible.

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1Based on our projections, we are going to expand the vendor network.Com base em nossas projeções, vamos expandir a rede de fornecedores.
2By the end of Q1, we will have completed the migration.Até o final do Q1, teremos completado a migração.
3The pilot program is due to launch in February.O programa piloto está previsto para fevereiro.
4I recommend we bring forward the testing phase to close the gap.Recomendo antecipar a fase de testes para fechar a lacuna.
5We are on track, but we may need to accelerate the procurement cycle.Estamos no caminho, mas talvez precisemos acelerar o ciclo de aquisição.
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Reading the Room -- Relative Clauses in Cross-Cultural Negotiations

Advanced relative clauses (who, which, that, whose, where; defining vs non-defining; reduced). New vocabulary: cross-cultural, high-context culture, low-context culture, save face, read between the lines, etiquette, directness, small talk, reciprocity, common ground, mediator, accommodate.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Cross-cultural -- involving two or more different cultures
"Cross-cultural negotiation requires more than language skills." (Intercultural)
High-context culture -- a culture where meaning is communicated indirectly
"Japan is a high-context culture, which means a lot goes unsaid." (Cultura de alto contexto)
Low-context culture -- a culture where meaning is stated openly and directly
"Germany is a low-context culture, where directness is respected." (Cultura de baixo contexto)
Save face -- to protect someone's dignity in front of others
"Never correct a partner publicly; let them save face." (Salvar as aparências)
Read between the lines -- to understand a hidden or indirect meaning
"When they said maybe, I had to read between the lines." (Ler nas entrelinhas)
Etiquette -- the unwritten rules of polite, correct behavior
"Business etiquette varies enormously between countries." (Etiqueta)
Directness -- being clear, frank, and straightforward
"In some cultures, directness is a sign of respect." (Franqueza / objetividade)
Small talk -- light, informal talk before serious business
"In Brazil, small talk builds the relationship before the deal." (Conversa informal)
Reciprocity -- exchanging favors for mutual benefit
"Reciprocity matters: a concession should be returned." (Reciprocidade)
Common ground -- shared interests both sides can agree on
"We found common ground on delivery timelines first." (Pontos em comum)
Mediator -- a neutral person who helps two sides agree
"The mediator who joined us helped adjust our pace." (Mediador)
Accommodate -- to adjust your behavior to fit another's style
"A good negotiator accommodates the other side's pace." (Acomodar-se / adaptar-se)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Cross-cultural
High-context culture
Low-context culture
Save face
Read between the lines
Etiquette
Directness
Small talk
Reciprocity
Common ground
Mediator
Accommodate

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Cross-cultural negotiation is an art. The manager who leads a meeting in a high-context culture rarely says no directly. A response that sounds like maybe is often a polite refusal, which you have to read between the lines. Silence, which many Westerners find awkward, is actually a sign of careful thinking. The senior manager whose approval the team needs usually speaks last. Etiquette matters enormously here, and the colleague who introduces you sets the tone. In a low-context culture, the buyer who leads the meeting gets straight to the numbers, which can surprise people used to small talk. The negotiator who reads the room, and who accommodates the culture in front of them, is the one who builds common ground. Tokyo, where the first deal was signed, taught the team the value of reciprocity.

1. In "the manager who leads a meeting", what does "who" refer to?
A A person -- "who" is used for people (defining clause).
B A thing -- "who" is used for objects.
C A place -- "who" is used for locations.
2. Why is "which" (not "that") used in "Silence, which many find awkward, ..."?
A Because "silence" is a person.
B It is a non-defining clause (extra info, with commas) -- which is required, never that.
C Because the sentence is in the past.
3. What does "whose" express in "the manager whose approval the team needs"?
A Possession -- the approval belongs to the manager.
B A place where something happens.
C The same as "who is".

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Relative Clauses

GRAMMAR

Pronomes relativos para descrever pessoas, coisas e lugares com precisão. / Relative pronouns to describe people, things, and places with precision.

Pronoun / PronomeUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
who / thatPessoas / PeopleThe manager who leads the call.
which / thatCoisas e ideias / ThingsThe deal that closes.
whosePosse / PossessionThe manager whose approval we need.
where / whenLugares e tempos / Places, timesThe room where terms are agreed.
Non-defining (com vírgulas)Info extra; use which, nunca that / Extra info, never thatSilence, which they find odd, is normal.
Ponto-chaveVírgulas = clause não-definidora (info extra, use which/who). Sem vírgulas = clause definidora (essencial).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"The negotiator reads the room builds trust."
"The senior manager, approval we need, will speak last."
"Directness, can feel blunt, is respected in Germany."
"This is the table the final terms were agreed."
"In a high-context culture, you have to ."
"A good negotiator the other side's pace."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the events in the correct order.

?He warns the team to read between the lines and respect saving face.
?Marcus gives the team a cultural briefing before the call.
?The team builds common ground first, and the deal closes successfully.
?Fabiana leads the call and lets the local colleague introduce everyone.
?He explains that Japan is a high-context culture where meaning is indirect.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

The negotiator who reads the room is the one who builds trust.
O negociador que lê o ambiente é aquele que constrói confiança.
In a high-context culture, you have to read between the lines.
Em uma cultura de alto contexto, você precisa ler nas entrelinhas.
The senior manager, whose approval we need, will speak last.
O gerente sênior, cuja aprovação precisamos, falará por último.
Let me build some common ground before we discuss the numbers.
Deixe-me construir pontos em comum antes de discutirmos os números.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
